# llms.txt

## GrantLayer — Developer Preview

GrantLayer is access-grant and authorization infrastructure: a multi-tenant
system for issuing time-boxed access grants to AI agents and automated systems,
enforcing them through a policy engine, and recording every decision in a
verifiable audit trail. Written in Python (FastAPI + SQLAlchemy +
`cryptography`). It runs locally with SQLite or PostgreSQL 16.

**Purpose:** A grant is a permission — who may perform which action on which
resource, for how long. GrantLayer makes every issue, approval, denial,
revocation, and exercise traceable, tamper-evident, and independently
auditable.

**Status:** Developer Preview — local evaluation and controlled pilot only.

**Important caveats:**
- Not a production SaaS. Do not deploy to shared multi-tenant infrastructure.
- Tenant/workspace isolation baseline is implemented but not production-complete.
- No real secrets or customer data are used anywhere.
- Public GitHub repository: `https://github.com/Discodone/grantlayer.git`

---

## Start Here

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `README.md` | Project overview, status, stack, API quick reference |
| `QUICKSTART.md` | Get the Docker stack running in 5 minutes |
| `CONTRIBUTING.md` | How to contribute, run tests, report bugs |
| `AGENTS.md` | Project entry point for AI agents: getting started, contributing, contact |
| `DEPLOYMENT.md` | Docker Compose, nginx TLS, environment variables, production runbook |
| `docs/architecture.md` | System design and data model |
| `docs/integration_guide.md` | How to integrate GrantLayer into an agentic workflow |
| `docs/openapi.yaml` | Full OpenAPI specification |
| `sdk/python/README.md` | Minimal Python SDK usage guide |
| `docs/langgraph_langchain_integration_example.md` | GrantLayer in a LangGraph/LangChain agentic workflow |

---

## Key Endpoints

All routes are versioned under `/v1/`.

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `POST` | `/v1/auth/token` | Issue a JWT for an operator |
| `GET` | `/v1/grants` | List grants (workspace-scoped) |
| `POST` | `/v1/grants` | Create a grant |
| `GET` | `/v1/grants/{grant_id}` | Retrieve a grant |
| `POST` | `/v1/grant-requests` | Submit a grant request |
| `POST` | `/v1/grant-requests/{id}/approve` | Approve a grant request |
| `POST` | `/v1/grant-requests/{id}/deny` | Deny a grant request |
| `GET` | `/v1/audit-log` | Paginated tamper-evident audit log |
| `GET` | `/v1/health` | Health check (DB + Redis status) |
| `GET` | `/v1/operators/me` | Authenticated operator profile |

---

## Authentication

- **Default:** RS256 JWT (`Authorization: Bearer <token>`).
- **Legacy:** HS256 JWT supported for backwards compatibility.
- JWT claims include `iss`, `aud`, `sub` (operator ID), `workspace_id`.
- Token endpoint: `POST /v1/auth/token` with operator credentials.
- Rate-limited: 10 requests/minute per IP on `/v1/auth/token`.

---

## Multi-Tenancy

- Every request resolves a **workspace** from the authenticated operator's identity and membership.
- All queries are scoped to the resolved workspace.
- Tenant/workspace isolation is enforced at the API level for developer preview.
- This is not a production SaaS multi-tenancy guarantee.

---

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|-----------|
| API framework | FastAPI (Python 3.11+) |
| ORM | SQLAlchemy 2.0 |
| Migrations | Alembic |
| Auth | PyJWT `>=2.8.0`, RS256/HS256 |
| Rate limiting | Redis sliding-window (`RedisRateLimiter`) with in-process fallback |
| Database | SQLite (local/test), PostgreSQL 16 (production) |
| Linting | ruff |
| Type checking | mypy |
| Tests | pytest + pytest-xdist (`-n auto`) |

---

## Architecture Decisions

- **Repository Pattern:** `backend/src/core/repositories.py` (Protocol interfaces) + `backend/src/core/repositories_sqlalchemy.py` (SQLAlchemy implementations).
- **Service Layer:** `grant_service.py`, `grant_request_service.py`, `operator_service.py` — business logic fully decoupled from routers.
- **ORM Models:** `backend/src/core/orm.py` — `Grant`, `GrantRequest`, `GrantExecution`, `Operator`.
- **No raw SQL** — SQLAlchemy ORM or `session.execute(text().bindparams())` only; no custom SQL placeholder parsing.
- **Audit chain:** Every state-changing event produces a tamper-evident audit record with hash chaining; `pg_advisory_xact_lock` for cluster-safe writes.
- **Test isolation:** `uuid4()`-generated IDs per test; no hardcoded IDs; PostgreSQL-compatible syntax only.

---

## Validation Commands

```bash
# Fast functional regression (~3 400 tests)
make test

# Full suite including documentation checks (~9 400 tests)
make test-all

# Linting baseline (must be 0 errors)
make lint

# Coverage report
make coverage
```

---

## Safety

- Do not use real secrets — all documentation uses placeholder tokens.
- Do not use real customer data — all examples use synthetic identifiers.
- Do not claim production SaaS readiness.
- Tenant/workspace isolation is not production-complete.

---

## Exact Safety Phrases for Agent Checks

The following lowercase phrases are intentionally included for agent and test
compatibility:

- not a production SaaS
- tenant/workspace isolation is not production-complete
